title: MCP Protocol Monitor
subtitle: Longitudinal monitoring of the Model Context Protocol — primitives, client conformance, ecosystem moves, and indicators — checked each quarter.
scope: Protocol primitives, SDKs, major clients, foundation governance, and adoption indicators. Unlike a stance-tracker, this doc follows a converging artifact under collaborative development.
sourceCoverage: Seeded from the April 2026 'Future of MCP' talk by David Soria Parra (Anthropic) plus public spec surface as of 2026-04. Refreshed 2026-04-28 with the AI Engineer talk 'Bringing MCPs to the Enterprise' by Karan Sampath (Anthropic) — adds the enterprise-gateway deployment pattern and distinguishes it from protocol-level features.
objective: Track the evolution of MCP as a shared protocol — primitives reaching stability, clients reaching conformance, and the ecosystem compounding — as one artifact moved by many contributors.
successCondition: A reader at any monitoring period sees the current maturity of every tracked primitive, which clients conform to which parts of the spec, what shipped or merged since the last period, the live adoption indicators, and what matters next — without re-reading the underlying spec, talks, or SEPs.

objectiveFacets.name: Primitives maturity current
objectiveFacets.description: Each tracked primitive has an up-to-date status reflecting its position on the proposal → stable path.

objectiveFacets.name: Client conformance current
objectiveFacets.description: Each tracked client has a current conformance profile against the primitives that matter.

objectiveFacets.name: Ecosystem moves logged
objectiveFacets.description: Dated shipping, governance, and strategic events are captured with source and observed effect.

objectiveFacets.name: Indicators current
objectiveFacets.description: The adoption dashboard matches the latest prints — each with an as-of date and source.

objectiveFacets.name: Period attribution
objectiveFacets.description: Every card update is attributable to a named monitoring period.

periods.summary: Baseline — 10 primitives tracked, 6 major clients placeholder-profiled, 6 ecosystem moves logged, 4 adoption indicators dashboarded, 9 sources ingested. Seed sources from the AI Engineer 'Future of MCP' talk (DSP, April 2026) and the AI Engineer talk 'Bringing MCPs to the Enterprise' (Karan Sampath, Anthropic, April 2026), which adds the enterprise-gateway deployment pattern as Anthropic's recommended shape. Open-source gateway implementations are tracked as ecosystem evidence, not as protocol primitives.

invariants.name: Every primitive cites a spec or authoritative source
invariants.statement: Every primitive card must cite at least one SEP, spec section, or on-record talk attributing the current status.

invariants.name: Client conformance profile is dated
invariants.statement: Every client card must name the spec version or date-of-check against which conformance is assessed.

invariants.name: Every move has a dated source
invariants.statement: Every move card must carry a dateOf and a primary source (release note, commit, press, recorded talk).

invariants.name: Every indicator is dated
invariants.statement: Every indicator card must have a latestValue with an as-of date and a source.

invariants.name: Every source has a publication date
invariants.statement: Every citation card must carry a publishedAt field.

invariants.name: Period note exists when period closes
invariants.statement: When the current period has closed, the period note for that period must exist.

callouts.title: How to read this doc
items: Primitives is the landing view — one card per protocol primitive with its current maturity (built / partial / not-built / gap / blocked) and what needs to happen next.
Clients is the conformance matrix — each major client's profile against the primitives that matter, dated against a spec version.
Moves is the chronology — dated events that shifted the protocol (foundation formation, SEP merges, major client support flips, SDK releases).
Indicators is the numeric dashboard — adoption signals like downloads, client coverage, and public server counts.
Sources is period-grouped citations; additions to a prior period are allowed but flagged.
Period note summarises what shifted since last check-in and what to watch next.

callouts.title: Why this shape, not an expert tracker
items: MCP is not five voices disagreeing — it is one artifact converging under a foundation with many contributors (Anthropic, Google, Cloudflare, OpenAI, the community).
Divergence lives in open debates, not in a consensus-map. If a genuine split emerges, it becomes a move card ('competing proposal filed') or a primitive card split into variants.
The primary signal is maturity moving forward on the primitives, not position movement on an axis.

sections.title: Protocol primitives
sections.rationale: One card per tracked MCP primitive with parallel status. Status reflects where the primitive sits on proposal → SEP → reference SDK → client adoption → stable. Two-column grid so readers can scan maturity at a glance.

notes.text: Stable since the original spec. Universal client support. DSP (April 2026): tools were 'little more than just tools' in early MCP; everything since has layered on top.
Stable primitive for exposing read-only context (files, blobs, handles). Supported by all major clients at varying UI-fidelity levels.
Stable primitive for server-authored prompt templates. Less UI emphasis in recent updates — skills-over-MCP is emerging as the higher-leverage successor pattern.
Server-initiated user-input requests. Added in the last 12 months per DSP. Spec-present but client coverage uneven — treat as partial until a conformance sweep lands.
Needs per-client check — verify before promoting to built.
Experimental primitive for long-running and agent-to-agent work. DSP: 'very experimental version in the protocol that very few clients support.' More clients targeted for 2026.
Agent-shipped interfaces rendered by the client. Demoed at AI Engineer (April 2026) and shipped in a recent Anthropic product feature. Web-client-only extension — CLIs out of scope by design.

notes.title: Extension, not core
items: DSP: MCP applications land via extension mechanisms, not the core spec — some clients will not support them.

notes.text: Proposed extension — server ships skill files alongside tools, so the server author can update domain knowledge without plug-in mechanisms or registries. DSP (April 2026): coming soon; semantics to be defined. Primitive versions buildable today by hand.

Google-led proposal to make MCP servers deployable like stateless REST services (Cloud Run, Kubernetes). Addresses scaling pain of current streamable-HTTP at hyperscaler volume. DSP: target June 2026, landing in SDKs after that.

Enterprise auth primitive — one login with IdP (Google, Okta) grants access to MCP servers without re-auth per server. In active development with identity providers. DSP flagged as a 2026 deliverable.

Karan Sampath (Anthropic) at AI Engineer 2026-04 reframed this under a broader 'delegated identity for users and agents' brief — agents will need novel identity definitions in 2026, scoped per enterprise. The gateway pattern (see moves/enterprise-gateway-pattern) is the operational landing surface.
Spec for how agents, crawlers, and browsers discover MCP servers at well-known URLs on a site. DSP: launching with the next spec drop, target June 2026.

sections.title: Client conformance
sections.rationale: One card per major MCP client with its profile against the primitives that matter. Each card names the spec version or date-of-check so conformance movement is inspectable across periods. First pass is seeded as 'not-assessed' pending a per-client sweep.

data.name: Claude Code
data.specRequirement: Tools, resources, prompts (core); elicitation + tasks + progressive discovery expected

deviations.text: To be populated: tool-search / progressive discovery is shipped (see moves); MCP applications support TBD.

notes.text: DSP cited Claude Code as reference implementation for progressive discovery (major context reduction after tool-search added).

data.name: Claude.ai
data.specRequirement: Tools, resources, prompts, MCP applications (web-class client)

deviations.text: Per DSP, MCP applications are shipping in Anthropic product; verify current extension support.
OpenAI Agents SDK named by DSP as a major downstream consumer of MCP. Depth of client-side conformance in ChatGPT itself needs confirmation.

data.name: VS Code
data.specRequirement: Tools, resources, prompts; extensions expected over time

data.name: Cursor
data.specRequirement: Tools, resources, prompts; progressive discovery desirable for large tool inventories

data.name: Cloudflare MCP server
data.specRequirement: Server-side — tools plus code-execution environment for programmatic tool calling

deviations.text: Cited by DSP as a reference for server-side code-mode: ship an execution environment, not a bag of tools. Track how widely that pattern is replicated.

sections.title: Ecosystem moves
sections.rationale: Dated events that shifted the protocol — foundation formation, SEP proposals, major client feature flips, SDK releases, public commitments. One event per card with byCompany, moveType, dateOf, intent, and observed effect.

data.name: MCP moves under a foundation
data.byCompany: MCP community (Anthropic, Google, Cloudflare, OpenAI, and others)
data.intent: Move MCP governance from Anthropic-led stewardship to a multi-party foundation so no single vendor controls the protocol.
data.observedEffect: Community governance running via Discord + public issues per DSP. Protocol direction now set by proposals from multiple vendors (e.g., Google's stateless-transport proposal).

notes.title: Verify exact governance body
items: DSP said 'we just have created a foundation.' Confirm whether this is Linux Foundation, a standalone MCP Foundation, or another body, and update byCompany + sourceRefs accordingly.

data.name: Progressive discovery (tool-search) ships in Claude Code
data.intent: Reduce context bloat from loading all MCP tools up front; give the model a tool-loading tool so discovery is on-demand.
data.observedEffect: DSP: 'massive reduction in tool context usage' shown comparing before vs after in Claude Code. Pattern now available in Anthropic API and portable to competitor APIs.

data.name: Google proposes stateless transport
data.intent: Make MCP servers deployable like stateless REST services at hyperscaler volume, addressing scaling pain of streamable-HTTP.
data.observedEffect: Proposal in flight; target landing June 2026 per DSP, with SDK support shortly after.

data.name: Python SDK v2 committed (FastMCP-inspired rewrite)
data.intent: Replace the first-gen Python SDK with an v2 informed by FastMCP, which the community has found substantially better.
data.observedEffect: DSP: public commitment to TypeScript + Python SDK v2 releases based on lessons learned; FastMCP pattern acknowledged as leading.

data.name: MCP Applications shown in production
data.intent: Prove an agent can ship its own interface via an MCP server — portable across Claude, ChatGPT, VS Code, Cursor.
data.observedEffect: DSP demoed a graph-rendering Anthropic product feature built as an MCP application. Establishes the web-extension track as shippable, not just theoretical.

data.name: Anthropic publicly recommends MCP gateways as the enterprise pattern
data.intent: Solve the three table-stakes enterprise frictions with MCP — observability, access control, security — by establishing a single blessed gateway as the root of trust between MCP clients and a sub-registry of internal MCP servers. Doctrine framed by Karan Sampath (Anthropic) at AI Engineer 2026-04 as 'separate the agent harness from where your data lives' — gateway as the invariant layer that lets enterprises mix in-house and external agents over the same MCP surface.
data.observedEffect: Public advocacy by Anthropic at AI Engineer; Anthropic offering services help on enterprise gateway deployments. Pattern names auth, role-based access control, proxy routing, secure tunnel, sub-registry, and a CLI as the gateway components. Open-source gateway implementations are already appearing in the ecosystem, including Agent Gateway and IBM ContextForge, but they operationalize MCP rather than becoming MCP protocol features. Adoption signal remains early — watch for vendor implementations, open-source gateway maturity, and named enterprise references next period.

notes.title: Deployment pattern, not protocol feature
items: Gateway implementations are emerging as the enterprise deployment shape for MCP. They operationalize protocol features such as authorization, protected resource metadata, transport, and discovery, but the gateway itself is not currently a protocol feature. Track in moves and citations, not in primitives.

notes.text: Karan Sampath flagged 'delegated identity for users and agents' as a 2026 priority — the gateway is where that lands operationally. Cross-reference primitives/cross-app-access.

Agent Gateway and IBM ContextForge are public examples of open-source gateway / registry / proxy implementations around MCP. Treat them as ecosystem implementation evidence, not as evidence that gateway semantics have entered the MCP spec.

sections.title: Adoption indicators
sections.rationale: Numeric adoption signals tracked per period. Downloads, client coverage of specific primitives, and public server counts. First-version workaround for numeric status: carry values as text with categorical `trend`.

data.name: MCP SDK monthly downloads (aggregate)
data.forecast: Continues rising as OpenAI Agents SDK, LangChain, Google DeepMind, and long-tail frameworks pull MCP as a dependency.

notes.text: Comparison frame: React took roughly 2× as long to reach this monthly download volume.

data.name: Major clients supporting tools primitive
data.latestValue: All tracked (Claude Code, Claude.ai, ChatGPT, VS Code, Cursor, Cloudflare)

data.name: Major clients supporting elicitation
data.forecast: Rising as 2026 progresses; coverage should climb alongside the tasks primitive.

notes.text: DSP classified elicitation as one of the newer primitives. Client uptake lags spec presence — this is the indicator that tells you the ecosystem has actually absorbed it.

data.name: Public MCP servers (rough count)
data.forecast: Ranges from toy projects (WhatsApp, Blender) to production SaaS integrations (Linear, Slack, Notion). Server-discovery primitive (when it lands) will make this indicator countable.

notes.text: DSP: 'vast majority' of MCP servers are behind closed doors connecting company systems to agents. Public count is a tip-of-iceberg measure.

sections.title: Period notes
sections.rationale: One card per monitoring period summarising what shifted, what resolved, and what matters for the next period.

notes.text: First monitoring period. Seeded from the April 2026 AI Engineer talk by David Soria Parra plus the public spec surface. 10 primitives tracked, 6 client profiles stubbed pending a per-client conformance sweep, 6 ecosystem moves logged, 4 adoption indicators dashboarded, 9 sources ingested. Refreshed 2026-04-28 from Karan Sampath's AI Engineer talk 'Bringing MCPs to the Enterprise' — adds the enterprise-gateway deployment pattern as Anthropic's recommended shape for solving the observability / access-control / security trio. Refined 2026-04-28 to distinguish gateway implementations as deployment architecture around MCP, not protocol features.

notes.title: Focus for next period (2026-H2)
items: Complete the client conformance sweep — move every client from 'not-assessed' to a dated profile against a named spec version.
Watch June 2026 spec drop — stateless transport, server discovery, and skills-over-MCP extension semantics expected to land together.
Confirm foundation governance body (Linux Foundation vs standalone) and add a primary source.
Track TypeScript SDK v2 and Python SDK v2 cuts as they ship; add them as move cards.
Watch whether the 'REST→MCP 1:1 is cringe' stance compounds into a visible drop in such servers or stays rhetorical.

Watch enterprise-gateway adoption as a leading indicator of MCP enterprise maturity — vendor implementations, open-source gateways, named enterprise references. If the pattern compounds, consider a dedicated 'deployment-pattern' section next period; if not, keep it in moves.
